Just in: U.S. stock market pre-market: All three major stock index futures rose, technology stocks rebounded strongly, and Brent oil returned to above $90.

Before the market opened on Tuesday, all three major U.S. stock index futures rose, and major European indexes generally rose. As of press time, Nasdaq 100 futures were up 1.31%, Dow futures were up 0.23%, and S&P 500 futures were up 0.41%. In terms of commodities, Brent crude oil rose 1.67% to US$90.71/barrel; WTI crude oil rose 1.66% to US$83.85/barrel.

Spot gold rose 1.41% to $4,065.23 an ounce. Spot silver rose 4.78% to $59.1. In terms of individual stocks, memory chip stocks generally rose, with SanDisk rising by more than 7%, Western Digital and SK Hynix rising by more than 6%, and Micron Technology and Seagate Technology rising by more than 5%.

Most popular Chinese concept stocks rose before the market opened. UMC rose nearly 8%, TSMC rose nearly 4%, https://t.co/b2YiKu12vt rose nearly 3%, NIO and https://t.co/6YYIAsUuRm rose nearly 1%, and Baidu and Alibaba fell nearly 1%. Geographically, the exchange of fire between the United States and Iran continues.

According to a report by Xinhua News Agency citing Iran's Tasnim News Agency on the 21st, an explosion occurred at the U.S. Navy's Fifth Fleet headquarters in Bahrain. It was also reported that an alarm was sounded in Manama, the capital of Bahrain, and residents were asked to stay in shelters.

Oil prices turned higher, with Brent crude rising more than 1% to above $90. The earnings season for U.S. technology giants officially kicks off this week. Tesla and Alphabet will be the first to disclose their results on Wednesday, while Microsoft, Meta, Apple and Amazon will announce their financial reports next week.

Ikuo Mitsui, fund manager at Aizawa Securities in Tokyo, said: "The market has experienced a considerable adjustment. At the same time, corporate earnings performance has been generally solid and more resilient than expected." Technology stocks rebound violently, but Citigroup pours cold water: The elimination of U.S.

stock positions is far from over Asia-Pacific and U.S. stock technology sectors enjoyed a strong rebound on Tuesday, but Citigroup warned that the process of resetting U.S. stock positions has not yet been completed and investors should not relax prematurely.

A team of Citi strategists headed by David Chew pointed out that the recent sharp sell-off in artificial intelligence and technology stocks has triggered extensive de-risking operations. Fund flows in large-cap U.S. stocks have been overwhelmingly bearish, and the overall position has deteriorated significantly.

Among them, all long positions on the Nasdaq 100 Index are currently at a loss, and the position level is still high, posing potential downside risks. The warning comes as the market is undergoing a clear sentiment recovery. Memory chip stocks generally rose before the U.S. stock market opened on Tuesday.

As of press time, SanDisk rose more than 7%, Western Digital and SK Hynix rose more than 6%, and Micron Technology and Seagate Technology rose more than 5%. Asia-Pacific stock markets rose across the board, ending the previous four consecutive trading days of decline. The Korea Composite Index once expanded to 4%.

The Korean Exchange even activated the Sidecar mechanism due to excessive gains and suspended KOSPI programmed buying.

Citi specifically pointed out the risks of the Nasdaq 100: all current long positions have fallen into deep losses, and the overall position level is still in a relatively high range, which means that the pressure for further elimination has not yet been completely released.

UBS: Gold is still facing downward pressure in the short term, and it can increase its holdings if it falls back to $3,850 UBS said in its latest research report that gold is currently hovering around US$4,000 per ounce, and the re-escalation of military conflicts in the Middle East and rising oil prices are posing new pressures.

Rising oil prices may push up inflation and interest rate expectations, and the market is still digesting the Fed's hawkish signal. Gold's short-term trend depends on whether investment demand can pick up. UBS pointed out that although central bank gold purchases have increased again, they are not enough to drive gold prices up alone.

It is expected that global central bank gold purchases will reach 750 to 1,000 tons in 2026, and the long-term demand for reducing holdings of US dollar assets will provide support for gold prices; however, for gold to continue to stand above $4,000, stronger investment capital inflows are still needed.

UBS estimates that quarterly investment demand needs to be close to 500 tons to significantly push up gold prices. If the U.S. economy performs better than expected, the job market tightens and oil prices continue to rise, interest rates are expected to rise further, and gold prices may fall back to $3,850 or even lower.

However, UBS believes that weak investment demand is only a temporary phenomenon, and the drop in gold prices to around $3,850 should be seen as an opportunity to increase holdings rather than a reason to turn pessimistic.

If financial market uncertainty does not lead to expectations of interest rate cuts, the boost to gold will usually be difficult to sustain. UBS predicts that as consumer spending, real wage growth and artificial intelligence-related investments gradually slow down, the Federal Reserve may cut interest rates again in March 2027.

By then, interest rate revaluation, a weakening US dollar and demand for asset diversification are expected to drive gold back to strength.

Goldman Sachs: If the Strait of Hormuz continues to be blocked, Brent oil prices may exceed $120 in the fourth quarter Goldman Sachs said in a research report released on July 20 that if transportation in the Strait of Hormuz continues to be blocked, Brent crude oil prices may rise to more than $120 per barrel in the fourth quarter of this year.

However, this is not the bank's baseline scenario. A team of analysts led by Daan Struyven pointed out that the escalation of tensions in the Middle East and the fact that oil shipments in the Persian Gulf have dropped to less than 45% of pre-war levels have pushed international oil prices back higher.

Goldman Sachs still assumes that tensions in the Middle East will gradually ease, and accordingly predicts that Brent crude oil prices will average US$80 per barrel in the fourth quarter and average US$75 in 2027.

However, Goldman Sachs emphasized that due to continued disruptions to shipping in the Strait of Hormuz and potential disruptions to Red Sea shipping, the risks to its oil price forecast are clearly upward.

The Red Sea route was previously an important alternative channel for transporting related goods to customers after the Persian Gulf crude oil exports were blocked. Once this route is also affected, the pressure on global oil supply may further intensify.

The research report pointed out that the decline in global crude oil inventories in the second quarter means that the oil market’s buffer space against supply shocks has narrowed, and oil prices will become more sensitive to geopolitical risks.

For investors looking to hedge geopolitical risks in the Middle East and Russia, Goldman Sachs recommends going long the European diesel calendar spread from December 2026 to March 2027.

The bank believes that supply in the European diesel market was already tight before the outbreak of the war, and Ukraine's continued attacks on Russian refineries also increased the risk of refined oil supply.

TSMC's share price rises due to sudden changes, latest price increase news emerges According to the latest news on Tuesday, multiple industry chain figures told the media that TSMC plans to increase the price of chip production services by up to 10% from 2027, covering both advanced and mature process businesses, to reflect the rising costs of materials, manufacturing equipment, and construction of new chip factories.

It is reported that negotiations surrounding the price increase began in June this year and were finalized in July. The new pricing plan will take effect in early 2027. Among them, the basic increase including 7 nanometer and more advanced processes is between 5% and 10%, depending on the customer and product.

In the latest second quarter report, the advanced process business contributed 77% of revenue. It is worth mentioning that TSMC plans to charge a premium of 10% to 15% on top of the basic increase for additional orders of high-performance computing chips that exceed the initial forecasts of foundry customers.

Therefore, the overall price increase of some advanced chip orders may exceed 10%. In addition, prices covering 12nm, 16nm, 28nm and other mature process production will also be increased by up to 10%. Typically, the smaller the chip process, the more advanced the performance.

But for key electronic applications such as power management and sensors, mature process chips are still crucial. Stimulated by this news, TSMC's US stock ADR rose by more than 3% before the market opened. In the previous round of adjustments in July, TSMC fell by more than 16% from its historical high.

Deutsche Bank: Microsoft’s AI investment concerns have over-reflected that Azure’s growth rate may reach 41% Deutsche Bank said in its latest research report that the market's concerns about Microsoft's surge in capital expenditures, AI investment return rate and OpenAI order concentration have been significantly excessive, and the current valuation is attractive, maintaining a "buy" rating and a target price of $550.

Deutsche Bank estimates that Microsoft’s fourth fiscal quarter (corresponding to the second quarter of the natural year) revenue will be US$87.4 billion, a year-on-year increase of 14.3%; non-GAAP earnings per share will be US$4.17. Azure's growth rate is expected to reach 40% to 41% based on fixed exchange rates.

Enterprise cloud migration, AI computing power demand and new data center capacity will continue to support growth. Microsoft 365 commercial cloud revenue is expected to grow by 15% to 16%, and Copilot paid seats may exceed 25 million by the end of the year.

Affected by the rising prices of components such as memory and the expansion of AI infrastructure, Microsoft's fourth-quarter capital expenditure (including leasing) is expected to exceed US$40 billion, a record high; Deutsche Bank raised its capital expenditure forecast for fiscal year 2027 from US$215 billion to US$238 billion.

Microsoft's book free cash flow for the year may be close to breakeven, but it will still pay dividends of approximately US$26 billion. Deutsche Bank believes that Microsoft can alleviate profit margin pressure through AI infrastructure price increases, operating expense control and Azure revenue growth.

Deutsche Bank estimates that Microsoft's revenue and adjusted earnings per share in fiscal 2027 will be US$384.2 billion and US$19.45 respectively. Despite the pressure on short-term gross profit margins, Azure and Microsoft 365 can still drive the company's profits to maintain mid-double-digit growth.

On the eve of Google’s financial report, Deutsche Bank strongly supports: Don’t just focus on Capex, the real surprise may come from Google Cloud After the U.S. stock market closes on July 22, Google parent company Alphabet will release its second quarter 2026 financial report.

The current market trading logic is highly focused on a core proposition: whether the continued expansion of capital expenditures (Capex) in the AI era is a heavy burden that erodes profits, or is it a forward-looking realization of future growth.

Deutsche Bank pointed out in a report released on July 20 that although the market has recently been concerned about the slowdown in search business growth, the delay in the release of new Gemini models, and the continued increase in AI investment, the variable that really deserves attention is not the capital expenditure itself, but that Google Cloud revenue growth may significantly exceed current expectations.

If cloud business demand is released as scheduled, the incremental profits it will bring in the next few years will be enough to cover higher AI investment intensity, and the company's overall profitability still has room for further improvement.

For investors, the key point of this financial report is not whether Capex continues to rise, but how management sets the pace of cloud service demand, changes in order backlog, and future revenue realization. The above signals may determine whether the market will reprice Alphabet's valuation logic under the AI investment cycle.

Focusing on AI infrastructure construction, Deutsche Bank simultaneously significantly increased Alphabet's future capital expenditure forecast. The company had previously forecast capital expenditures of $180 billion to $190 billion in 2026, and said it would be "significantly higher" in 2027 than this year.

However, analysts also pointed out that because Google uses a hybrid deployment of TPU and Nvidia GPU, its unit construction cost is expected to be lower than market concerns.

ING: The UK’s autumn budget is expected to remain restrained and an early election may become the biggest risk to the market ING pointed out that after the new British Prime Minister Andy Burnham took office, the British bond market responded mutedly.

Although it advocates expanding investment in social housing and promoting nationalization, it has promised to abide by fiscal rules and not increase major taxes. The market is generally expected to have a relatively moderate budget this autumn.

Under the baseline scenario, the government may reduce bus fares and tax burdens on the hotel and catering industry, and transfer part of the energy policy costs from electricity charges to general taxes, in order to coordinate with the reduction of the energy price ceiling in October and suppress overall inflation.

However, the government still needs to fill a departmental spending gap of about £6 billion in 2029, as well as an unfinanced portion of the defense investment plan of £4.7 billion.

Adjustments to the fiscal framework are expected to free up an additional 16 billion pounds in borrowing space, enough to support limited increases in spending and moderate tax cuts.

Even so, UK government bond issuance is still expected to fall from 304 billion pounds in the previous fiscal year to 246 billion pounds this fiscal year, and ING expects the Bank of England to restart interest rate cuts in 2027.

The market risk is that Burnham launches a more radical plan, including expanding housing and infrastructure investment, reforming property taxes and adjusting fiscal rules.

Increasing the personal tax exemption by 10% would cost 11 billion pounds per year; replacing a 2 percentage point increase in income tax with a 2 percentage point reduction in national insurance would increase revenue by approximately 6 billion pounds.

If defense or investment spending is excluded from fiscal constraints, new debt issuance could still push gilt yields higher. ING believes that the biggest unexpected risk is an early election. The current probability given by the betting market is only 15% to 20%, but the reality may be higher.

A general election could lead to bolder borrowing plans or a policy stagnation after Labor loses its majority, making it difficult to welcome investors. Morgan Stanley: The pullback in U.S. memory chip stocks brings buying points as data center shortages continue to intensify Morgan Stanley said in its latest research report that the recent sell-off in U.S.

memory chip stocks has provided investors with attractive entry opportunities.

Although there are signs of slowing demand, rising capital expenditures, and downgrading of product configurations in some areas of the market, the shortage of data center memory continues to intensify, and this industry cycle cannot be judged according to the traditional storage cycle.

Joseph Moore, an analyst at the bank, pointed out that the core driving force of this memory boom cycle comes almost entirely from data center demand, and weak signals released by other end markets may constitute a "false alarm." Among the semiconductor targets covered by Morgan Stanley, the risk-return ratio of memory chip stocks is still lower than that of Nvidia and Broadcom, but the gap between the two is rapidly narrowing.

Morgan Stanley believes that the market has recently been worried about a second-order slowdown in memory price growth, manufacturers increasing capital expenditures, and customers controlling costs by reducing product configurations, but these changes were foreseeable a month ago.

Different from ordinary cycles, memory is increasingly becoming a key bottleneck in AI infrastructure construction and intelligent AI server CPU deployment, and the supply shortage has not yet been significantly alleviated.

According to the bank’s communication with data center purchasers, data center memory prices rose by at least 25% month-on-month in the third quarter, higher than previous forecasts by Morgan Stanley and third-party institutions. Although the increase has slowed down from the second quarter, the bank believes that this change is not unexpected.

Long-term supply agreements and reduced allocations may reduce the volatility of this price cycle and make price increases lower than previously excessive market expectations, but may prolong the duration of the industry boom. In the long term, a longer cycle duration may be more conducive to the performance of related stocks than short-term price surges.

Morgan Stanley also emphasized that market concerns about further intensification of memory shortages in 2027 and 2028 remain strong.

ING: A strong El Niño may hit at the end of the year and concerns about global food crisis are exaggerated ING said that a historically strong El Niño may occur at the end of 2026, but its impact on global agricultural supply and food prices may be lower than market concerns. The real risks are mainly concentrated in the Asia-Pacific region and some crops.

Data from the U.S.

National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration shows that sea temperature anomalies in key Pacific Ocean areas reached 1.5 degrees Celsius in June, the highest for the same period since 1982; the probability of El Niño continuing until early 2027 is 97%, and the probability of developing into a "very strong" event from October to December this year is 81%.

ING pointed out that the impact of previous moderate-intensity El Niños on the total output of major global crops has been generally limited, and food prices have not shown a steady rise.

Irrigation, drought-tolerant seeds and satellite monitoring have improved agricultural resilience: the proportion of global cultivated land equipped with irrigation facilities has increased from 11% in 1980 to 22% in 2023, and the actual proportion of irrigation has increased from 65% to nearly 83%; currently, cereal stocks are also relatively abundant, which can buffer short-term shocks.

However, the Asia-Pacific faces more significant regional risks. Australian wheat, Southeast Asian palm oil, Indian and Thai sugar, and rice production may decrease in many places. Restrictions on Peruvian anchovy fishing have pushed up fishmeal prices by more than 75% year-on-year, while feed typically accounts for 55% to 65% of aquaculture costs in Asia.

Monsoon rainfall in India is still 24% below normal, and the government has banned sugar exports until at least the end of September. Historically, Australia's wheat production fell by 58% in 2002/03 and 36% in 2023/24.

ING recommends that food and agricultural companies strengthen weather, inventory, logistics and policy monitoring, and reduce risks by increasing water and feed reserves, optimizing hedging and finding alternative raw materials.

Asia-Pacific companies may be under short-term pressure, while agricultural producers in other regions may benefit from rising prices and market share.

Goldman Sachs: WAIC’s popularity of embodied intelligence surges and industrial scenarios take the lead in commercialization Goldman Sachs pointed out in its latest research report that embodied intelligence will become the most prominent theme at the 2026 World Artificial Intelligence Conference.

There were more than 1,100 exhibitors at the conference, including 242 embodied intelligence companies, a year-on-year increase of 203%, including 92 humanoid robot companies; 208 embodied intelligence products were displayed on site, a year-on-year increase of 247%, and more than 300 humanoid robots participated in the demonstration, doubling the number from the previous year.

In terms of technology, the integration of "Visual-Language-Action Model" (VLA) and "World-Action Model" (WAM) has become an industry consensus. Through the combination of fast motion control and slow cognitive reasoning, task efficiency, physical space reasoning and cross-ontology generalization capabilities can be improved.

However, the model has not yet been truly "out-of-the-box", and commercial deployment still relies on specific scenario data collection and on-site fine-tuning. Data collection is shifting from traditional teleoperation to lightweight methods such as first-person video, handheld grippers and data gloves.

Goldman Sachs predicts that the amount of global non-ontology data will expand from about 10 million hours in 2026 to hundreds of millions of hours in the next three to five years. However, the industry is still divided on the importance of data scale and high-quality information such as tactile and force control.

The progress of commercialization is obviously divided.

Logistics sorting, warehousing and handling, and some structured industrial scenarios are closest to large-scale applications; retail scenarios are still in the "available but inefficient" stage, and home scenarios are constrained by safety, cost, reliability, and non-standard environments, and are farthest away from implementation.

In terms of supply chain, wheeled chassis is still the mainstream in the near future. The number of dexterous hand manufacturers has increased from 9 to 38 in 2025. The technical routes are highly fragmented, and competition and commercialization pressure are increasing.

Goldman Sachs recommends selected targets: give Inovance Technology and Sanhua Intelligent Holdings H shares a "buy" rating; Sanhua A shares, Green Harmonic and Best are "neutral"; MOONS Electric is rated "sell" due to high valuations and intensifying competition for coreless motors and smart hands.

Fairlife recently suffered a ransomware attack and suspended production, which may drag down revenue in the third and fourth quarters, but the business is expected to recover in the first half of 2027.

# AI消灭入门级科技岗位：当对冲基金抛售科技股，谷歌把Gemini"焊死"在芯片里

━━━━━━━━━━━

## 一、核心问题

2026年7月的科技行业正同时经历三场结构性冲击——劳动力市场、资本市场和基础设施。这三件事单独看都不算罕见，但当它们在同一周同时出现时，我们必须认真对待一个可能性：**AI正在从"辅助工具"进化为"替代力量"，冲击的不只是某一家公司或某一个岗位，而是整个科技行业的底层逻辑。**

这不是渐进式的变革，而是多线并发的系统性重构。过去十年，"买入一切AI"是华尔街最成功的投资叙事。但就在同一个交易窗口里，高盛报告对冲基金以十年来最快速度抛售科技股，Anthropic研究员承认AI对就业的冲击仍处早期阶段，Alphabet宣布推出Frozen v2芯片将Gemini直接写入硅片，月之暗面Kimi K3发布48小时后用户过载被迫暂停注册。这些信号各自独立，却指向同一个方向——科技行业的三个维度正在被同时重塑。

核心数字如下：

> 22-25岁美国软件开发者就业同比下降 23%
> 科技行业对冲基金持仓占比降至 2026年2月以来最低
> Alphabet Frozen v2目标能效比现有TPU提升 6-10倍
> 微软评估Kimi K3替代ChatGPT/Claude，年省推理成本 $6亿

一个有趣的时间节点：2026年7月21日。AI CapEx仍在高速增长（Morgan Stanley预测2028年全球达到1.4万亿美元），但资本市场的信心已经出现裂缝。问题不是"AI有没有价值"，而是**AI的价值分配正在发生根本性转移**——从"所有科技公司都能分到一杯羹"转向"只有真正掌握AI能力基础设施的公司才能存活"。这正是我们今天要拆解的核心矛盾。

━━━━━━━━━━━

## 二、论据/Thesis

要理解这场结构性变革，我们需要把三个看似无关的信号串成一条因果链。

**第一个信号：AI正在消灭入门级技术岗位。** @KobeissiLetter引用的最新数据揭示了令人不安的代际分化——22-25岁美国软件开发者就业率同比下降23%，而41-49岁资深开发者反而上升18%。代际鸿沟达到41个百分点。这不是普通的周期波动，而是一个结构性拐点。

过去十年，科技行业的招聘逻辑是"金字塔模型"——大量初级工程师做基础工作，中层工程师做架构设计，资深工程师做决策。但AI工具（Claude Code、Cursor、GitHub Copilot、Google Gemini）的出现正在压缩这个金字塔的底端。当一个AI代理能够独立完成代码生成、单元测试、文档编写、甚至基础的系统架构审查时，企业对初级开发者的需求自然会下降。更深层的影响在于：**入门级岗位的消失意味着"学习曲线"本身被AI接管了**。年轻人不再需要通过写Bug来学习，因为AI帮他们规避了大部分错误——但这同时剥夺了他们通过实践成长的机会。这是一个悖论：AI让代码质量更高，却让人才培养更难。

**第二个信号：对冲基金创纪录地抛售科技股。** 高盛的交易员指出，科技行业在中性投资组合中的持仓占比已降至2026年2月以来最低。芯片股连续8天被卖出，Mag 7连续5周遭抛售。这不是因为AI没有价值，而是因为市场开始"去魅"。

"买入一切AI"时代的结束是一个重要标志。当投资者不再根据"我有AI功能"就愿意支付溢价时，市场正在回归基本面分析。对冲基金的逻辑是：如果一家公司的AI投入不能转化为可验证的收入增长和利润率改善，那么当前的估值就是泡沫。CLO权益层Q1回报-15%的数据进一步佐证了这个判断——信贷市场的压力正在表面之下加剧，软件贷款价格下跌叠加新企业贷款减少，说明实体经济的融资环境正在收紧。当融资成本上升时，"烧钱换增长"的AI叙事就面临考验。

**第三个信号：专用芯片的范式转移。** Alphabet内部代号"Frozen v2"的新芯片计划，代表了AI基础设施领域的最新方向——与其训练通用GPU来运行各种模型，不如把特定模型的推理逻辑直接固化到硅片中。谷歌的做法是将Gemini的模型架构"冻结"进芯片，目标是能效比现有TPU提升6-10倍，预计2028年部署。

这看似是谷歌的独家创新，实际上反映了一个更大的产业趋势：**通用算力正在让位于专用算力**。当推理成本降到足够低（每月订阅$20就能使用Claude或GPT-4o级别的模型），AI代理将从"实验性产品"变成"基础设施级服务"。这意味着科技行业的工作流程将发生根本性变化——不是"人用AI"，而是"AI驱动人的工作流程"。

**三条信号的共同逻辑：AI正在从"增量价值创造"转向"存量替代"。** 过去，AI为科技公司创造了新的收入来源（AI功能订阅、AI广告优化）；现在，AI正在替换科技公司内部的运营成本（初级员工、基础设施投入）。这种从"开源"到"节流"的转变，正是资本市场开始重新定价科技股的深层原因。

━━━━━━━━━━━

## 三、反方观点/Counterarguments

为了保持分析的客观性，我们必须认真审视反对上述Thesis的证据和逻辑。

**反方论点一：AI只是效率工具，历史证明它会创造更多就业。** 支持者引用经济史中的数据指出，每一次技术革命都经历了"短期失业、长期就业增加"的模式。19世纪的纺织机确实导致手工织工失业，但随后创造了纺织厂工人、机械师、设计师等新职业。20世纪的个人电脑普及也消灭了打字员岗位，但同时创造了软件开发者、IT支持、产品经理等大量新职业。

更具体地说，AI可能正在做的事情不是"消灭工作"，而是"重新定义工作"。初级开发者的工作内容可能从"手写每一行代码"转向"审查AI生成的代码、设计系统架构、协调跨团队交付"。这些岗位不仅需要技术能力，还需要沟通、判断和管理能力——而这恰恰是AI目前最难替代的领域。Anthropic的研究员在3月接受采访时表示，AI对就业的冲击"仍处于非常早期阶段"，暗示未来的演变可能比当前数据所显示的更加温和。

**反方论点二：对冲基金的抛售是战术调整，而非战略转向。** 批评者认为，高盛报告中提到的对冲基金抛售科技股，可能只是短期风控行为，而非对AI长期价值的否定。2026年Q2全球地缘政治风险急剧升高——美伊冲突升级、霍尔木兹海峡通行量暴跌至每天仅10艘船、布伦特原油突破90美元——这些因素足以解释任何理性的投资组合再平衡行为。

此外，标普500隐含相关性降至0.15的历史新低本身就说明，个股正在脱离大盘独立运行。在这种情况下，对冲基金抛售某些科技股，可能是因为这些个股的特定风险（估值过高、竞争加剧）被放大，而不是因为AI叙事整体崩塌。中国股票去杠杆的数据（沪深两市融资余额单日骤降117亿美元）也提醒我们，亚太市场的流动性收紧可能也在影响全球资金流向。科技股的回调可能是健康的去杠杆过程，为下一轮增长蓄力。

**反方论点三：Frozen v2芯片的赌注极大，可能成为"昂贵的废铁"。** 将模型架构直接固化到芯片中，意味着押注Gemini的模型结构在未来两年内不会发生重大变化。这是一种高度集中的技术路线选择。一旦Anthropic或OpenAI推出更高效的模型架构，或者谷歌自身决定调整Gemini的设计方向，Frozen v2的6-10倍能效优势可能瞬间缩水至2-3倍，甚至完全失去意义。

芯片行业的历史充满了"专用芯片失败"的案例。英伟达的早期竞争对手（如MIPS、SPARC）都曾试图用专用芯片挑战通用GPU，但最终都被证明缺乏灵活性。Google自己的TPU也从最初的争议中走来——早期批评者认为TPU只能运行TensorFlow，无法适配其他框架，结果TPU不仅活了下来，还成为云AI推理的核心基础设施。因此，Frozen v2的风险不是"能不能做到6-10倍能效"，而是"做到之后有没有足够的应用场景来覆盖研发成本"。

**证伪条件汇总：**

如果2027年Q1初级开发者就业回升超过10%，则"AI替代"Thesis不成立。
如果对冲基金在2026年底重新大规模买入科技股（持仓占比回到30%+），则"资本撤离"信号失效。
如果Frozen v2延期至2029年以后，或能效提升不足3倍，则"基础设施范式转移"推迟。
如果AI工具的使用率持续下降（企业削减AI订阅预算），则"AI渗透加速"前提不成立。

━━━━━━━━━━━

## 四、深度数据

让我们用多维度的数据来检验上述Thesis，并将其放在更广阔的产业背景中。

**第一层：AI就业冲击的行业细分数据**

• 初级软件开发（22-25岁）：就业下降23%。这是所有年龄段中受影响最大的群体。核心原因是，AI代码助手已经能够达到中级开发者的水平——Cursor能够根据自然语言描述生成完整功能模块，Claude Code能够完成代码审查和Debug，GitHub Copilot能够自动补全常见模式。

• 中级工程师（26-40岁）：就业基本持平，微幅下降约3-5%。这些岗位需要系统架构设计、数据库选型、跨团队协作和产品理解——AI目前还难以独立完成这些"模糊性高"的任务。

• 资深开发者（41-49岁）：就业上升18%。这是一个反向信号。AI时代的资深开发者不再是"写代码最快的人"，而是"知道什么时候不该写代码的人"。他们负责定义需求边界、评估技术方案的风险、管理AI代理的输出质量。

• 非技术岗位的影响：@jackhawksburn的帖子指出，给16岁孩子的AI建议正在过时——关键不是"学技术还是学非技术"，而是"执行能力 vs 判断力"。这个洞察很有价值：AI正在改变的不是"技术含量"的定义，而是"工作价值"的定义。

• 代际鸿沟：41个百分点的差距是历史性的。相比之下，1980年代互联网革命期间，不同年龄段的就业变化差距从未超过15个百分点。这意味着AI的冲击速度远超历史上任何一次技术变革。

**第二层：对冲基金抛售的科技股细分**

• 芯片股：连续8天被卖出。这并非偶然——半导体行业的库存周期可能正在进入下行阶段。Tower半导体计划在日本投资30亿美元扩产（获日本政府约10亿补贴），但这是产能扩张还是过剩预警，还需要观察下半年订单数据。

• Mag 7：连续5周遭抛售。原因包括估值过高（标普500中Mag 7权重曾经超过30%）、地缘风险（霍尔木兹海峡通航中断）、以及"AI叙事疲劳"。当所有公司都宣称自己有AI能力时，AI就失去了差异化竞争力。

• IT行业整体持仓：降至2026年2月以来最低。这说明抛售不是针对某个板块，而是系统性减仓。高盛交易员的评论"买入一切AI时代终结"非常有象征意义——它标志着华尔街对AI的态度从"无条件看好"转向"有条件验证"。

• CLO权益层Q1回报-15%：这是信贷市场压力的滞后指标。软件公司的贷款价格下跌叠加新企业贷款减少，说明银行对科技行业的风险偏好正在下降。这可能与对冲基金抛售形成联动：机构减持→股价下跌→抵押品价值下降→银行收紧信贷→企业融资更难。

• 标普500隐含相关性0.15：历史新低。个股正在脱离大盘独立运行，这意味着传统的"分散化投资"策略在科技股中失效。

**第三层：Frozen v2芯片的技术逻辑与产业含义**

• 现有TPU能效基准：当前Gemini模型在TPU v5p上的推理效率约为行业估算水平。Frozen v2的目标不是"小改进"，而是6-10倍量级的跃升。

• 实现路径：将Gemini的模型架构（注意力机制、前馈网络结构、归一化方案等）直接固化到硅片中，消除通用计算中的冗余指令。这类似于手机SoC将通信基带集成到芯片中的逻辑——专用硬件可以带来数量级的效率提升。

• 部署时间线：2028年。从现在到部署还有约2年的窗口期——足够Anthropic、OpenAI、月之暗面等竞争对手推出更高效的模型或架构。

• 风险敞口：专用芯片的"赌注"是模型架构的稳定性。如果Gemini在2028年前进行重大架构升级（例如从Transformer转向其他架构），Frozen v2可能需要推倒重来。更保守的估计是，能效提升可能只达到3-4倍而非6-10倍。

• 竞争格局：除了谷歌，英伟达也在推出Blackwell架构的专用推理芯片，IBM的Watsonx GPU和Amazon的Trainium同样在追赶。专用芯片市场竞争正在白热化。

**第四层：全球AI CapEx与竞争态势**

• Morgan Stanley预测2028年全球AI CapEx达到1.4万亿美元。这个数字反映了电信运营商、云厂商和科技巨头的集体押注。但值得注意的是，CapEx增长与收入增长的脱节正在扩大——如果收入增速跟不上CapEx增速，利润率将受到严重挤压。

• 微软评估Kimi K3替代ChatGPT/Claude，年省推理成本6亿美元。这是一个极其重要的信号：中国企业级AI的成本优势已经开始影响全球科技巨头的内容策略。如果Kimi K3能被证实具有与GPT-4o同等的推理能力且成本更低，那么"AI民主化"的叙事将进一步加速。

• 月之暗面Kimi K3发布48小时后用户过载暂停新用户。这个数据虽然来自社交媒体转述，但如果有真实流量支撑，它证明了中国AI模型的全球吸引力。@chuge857指出，这一事件支撑了AI CapEx长周期逻辑。

• 美国政府可能封禁Kimi K3的讨论（@bridgemindai引用）。这反映了中国在AI模型竞赛中的持续领先，以及进口管制博弈的升级。如果禁令实施，将影响全球AI开源生态和合作节奏。

━━━━━━━━━━━

## 五、场景推演

基于以上数据，我们构建三种情景，并为每种情景设定概率权重和关键指标。

**情景A：AI替代加速（概率40%）**

• 初级开发者岗位在2027年继续下降15-20%，累计降幅超过35%。更多企业从"招聘初级工程师"转向"招聘AI代理管理岗"。

• 对冲基金完成科技股去杠杆，IT行业持仓降至15%以下。标普500中的科技股权重从2024年的约30%回落至20%左右。

• Frozen v2如期于2028年部署，能效提升8倍以上。谷歌在AI推理市场的成本优势进一步拉开。

• 微软正式采用Kimi K3作为部分业务的替代方案，年省推理成本超过10亿美元。

• 结果：科技行业经历"创造性破坏"，AI-native公司（如月之暗面、Anthropic）崛起，传统科技公司面临重组。社会层面可能出现初级开发者的大规模转行需求，政策干预的可能性上升。

**情景B：AI渗透放缓（概率35%）**

• 初级开发者就业在2027年企稳，下降幅度收窄至5-10%，随后在2028年开始回升。原因——AI工具的可靠性不足以支撑全面替代，企业仍然需要人类工程师做最终审查。

• 对冲基金在2026年底重新买入科技股，持仓回升至25%。原因——地缘风险缓和、利率预期改善、AI收入增长超预期。

• Frozen v2延期至2029年，或因Gemini架构变更而取消。原因——AI模型架构的快速迭代使得"冻结"策略风险过高。

• Kimi K3未能通过微软的严格安全审查，全球推广受阻。

• 结果：科技行业维持现状，AI作为"辅助工具"而非"替代者"的定位得到验证。初级开发者的就业前景虽然不如2020年乐观，但不会经历断崖式下滑。

**情景C：AI泡沫破裂（概率25%）**

• AI CapEx增速大幅放缓，Morgan Stanley的1.4万亿美元预测被下调50%至7000亿美元。原因——企业发现AI投资的ROI难以量化，订阅收入增长不及预期。

• 对冲基金大规模撤离科技股，IT行业持仓降至10%以下。科技股经历类似2000年互联网泡沫的回调，但持续时间更长（3-5年）、恢复更慢。

• 初级开发者就业下降超过30%，引发政策干预（AI税、就业保护法规、强制企业保留一定比例的人类员工）。

• Frozen v2因资金缩减而被搁置。

• Kimi K3被美国正式列入禁售清单，中美AI脱钩加速。

• 结果：全球科技行业进入"后AI繁荣期"，类似2000-2004年的互联网寒冬。但不同的是，AI技术的底层能力仍在进步，只是商业化速度远慢于资本市场的预期。

**各情景下的关键指标：**

• 2026年Q4：对冲基金是否重新买入科技股（13F文件）
• 2027年Q2：初级开发者招聘广告数量是否触底回升
• 2027年Q4：Frozen v2的官方时间表是否确认
• 2028年Q1：全球AI CapEx实际增速是否达到Morgan Stanley预测
• 2028年Q3：微软是否正式宣布Kimi K3的企业级部署

━━━━━━━━━━━

## 六、观察信号

以下是6个可观察事件和指标，能够确认或证伪上述Thesis。建议在接下来的6-12个月内重点跟踪。

**信号一：初级开发者培训项目的存亡。** 大厂是否会减少或取消初级开发者培训计划？Google、Meta、Microsoft每年投入数十亿美元用于新员工培训。如果这些项目被削减或合并为"AI辅助培训"，则"AI替代"Thesis得到确认。反之，如果培训项目扩大且内容不变，则说明行业仍在"培养新人"。

**信号二：13F文件中的科技股持仓变化。** 每季度末的对冲基金13F披露是最权威的资本流向数据。重点关注：Vanguard、Berkshire Hathaway、Citadel、Renaissance Technologies等头部基金是否调整了科技股权重。如果IT行业整体配置在Q3回升至25%+，则"资本撤离"信号失效。

**信号三：Alphabet Q3/Q4财报电话会中对Frozen v2的表述。** 管理层是否会确认2028年部署时间线不变？是否会透露能效目标的具体进度？如果CEO Sundar Pichai在电话会上表示Frozen v2面临技术挑战或延期风险，则"基础设施范式转移" Thesis需要下调。

**信号四：中国AI模型的全球市场份额。** Kimi K3的用户过载和微软的成本评估是初步信号。如果2027年上半年有更多中国AI模型（月之暗面、智谱AI、百度文心等）进入欧洲、东南亚和拉美市场，则"AI去中心化" Thesis得到强化。反之，如果进口管制加强导致中国AI模型退出主流市场，则情景C的概率上升。

**信号五：CLO市场和科技行业贷款利差。** CLO权益层Q1回报-15%是滞后指标，但科技行业贷款的信用利差是先行指标。如果利差持续走阔，说明银行对科技企业的风险评估在恶化，这可能预示着更大的信贷紧缩。

**信号六：CPO/光模块等AI基础设施的实际订单。** 台积电、博通、Marvell等公司的AI相关订单是CapEx周期的领先指标。如果订单增速在2026年下半年明显放缓，则说明终端客户（云厂商）的AI投资意愿正在减弱，情景B或C的概率上升。

━━━━━━━━━━━

## 七、结论

AI正在同时重塑科技行业的三个层面——就业、资本、基础设施。这不是单一维度的变化，而是系统性重构。三个信号的交汇点不是巧合，而是同一棵因果树上的三根枝条。

**核心判断：**

• AI替代初级开发者的趋势已经确认，且短期内不可逆。代际鸿沟的41个百分点不是周期性波动，而是结构性拐点的早期信号。

• 对冲基金的抛售不是"看空AI"，而是"看空叙事"——市场正在从"AI万能"回归"AI现实"。那些无法证明AI投资回报率的科技公司将面临估值下调。

• Frozen v2代表了AI基础设施的下一个范式：专用芯片+固化模型=极致能效。但这条路线的赌注极高——一旦模型架构变化，专用芯片就失去意义。

**投资启示：**

• 关注AI基础设施领域的"卖铲人"（芯片、能源、数据中心）——无论哪家公司赢，都需要底层基础设施。

• 警惕"AI包装"公司——市场正在区分真AI和假AI。只有能证明AI带来可量化收入增长的公司才能获得估值溢价。

2026年7月20日盘后分析

美股进入关键转折周：宏观风险叠加财报考研，机构策略重心正在转向

2026年7月20日，美股步入了一个至关重要的时间节点。本周不仅面临美联储政策动向的持续影响，更有包括谷歌（Google）和特斯拉（Tesla）在内的多项科技巨头财报密集出炉。这些财报不仅将决定个股走势，更将考验目前相对脆弱的AI板块，尤其是AI硬件领域的韧性。

一、 宏观警报：地缘局势推动通胀风险重燃

目前市场最真金白银的风险信号来自于霍尔木兹海峡。受地缘冲突升级影响，航运保险公司已将该地区的战争险保费率推升至8%至10%，这达到了本轮冲突爆发以来的最高水平。

这种局势的恶化直接导致原油价格一度逼近90美元，并存在重回100美元大关的可能。对于美联储而言，这无疑是“致命打击”，可能引发加息预期的再度升温，从而使处于高位震荡的股指面临下行回落的巨大风险。

二、 机构策略转向：“重个股、轻大盘”的分散度交易

高盛最新的报告指出，目前期权市场的隐含相关性已接近20年来的最低水平。这预示着一个重大转变：分散度交易（Dispersion Trading）将成为年内机构的核心焦点。

这意味着市场走势将更多受个股微观因素驱动，而非宏观大环境。板块和个股之间的分化会愈发剧烈，选股逻辑将比赛道选择更加挑剔。在这一背景下，投资者应优先选择基本面强劲、现金流充足、且估值与技术面仍具上行空间的标的，并对高估值或技术面破位的风险个股及时锁定获利。

三、 AI板块内部分化：资金在轮转而非撤离

尽管AI芯片板块近期波动剧烈，但市场并未出现全面溃败。资金展现出了明显的内部轮转迹象：
资金流向： 从通用AI芯片向定制化芯片（如AVGO、Marvel）、内存芯片及上游设备商转移。
关键博弈： 市场正在屏息以待下周**微软（Microsoft）**的财报，特别是其对AI大规模资本支出合理性的解释，这将是决定AI投资情绪能否延续的关键。

四、 重点个股实战观察

ServiceNow (NOW)： 技术面表现稳健，已成功重回50日和100日均线上方，显示出强劲的反弹动能。期权市场对其财报持明显看涨态度，且其在软件股中具备较好的基本面前景，是目前相对安全的配置目标。
谷歌 (Google/GOOG)： 350美元是关键支撑位和高性价比切入点。目前股价处于低位反弹蓄势阶段，虽然长期基本面无虞，但期权市场情绪偏空，仓位过重者（超过40-50%）需警惕财报后的剧烈震荡。
美国运通 (AXP)： 尽管技术面在高位出现震荡回落信号，但期权市场对其未来一个月的走势极度看涨。由于其服务于高端客户，具备较强的抗风险能力，建议持有者等待财报表现。
防御性板块： 在现价角度，Costco (COST) 比刚破位的沃尔玛 (WMT) 更具吸引力，其底部反弹信号已初现，适合作为避险配置。

五、 总结与展望

当前大盘虽有偏空预期，但尚未出现恐慌性挤兑。板块赛道的持续轮转（如资金从AI硬件转向软件，或在芯片内部切换）起到了稳定器的作用。

接下来的操作核心应放在技术信号的确认上。许多高风险个股（如NBIS、Oracle、SRG等）目前仍处于测试底部或反弹信号尚不明确的阶段，盲目“抄底”风险极高。在当前震荡市中，保持交易纪律、关注个股微观逻辑优于盲目追逐大盘趋势。

Today's API crude inventory report lands at 4:30pm Eastern, the first read since the Strait of Hormuz disruption intensified. The setup going in is already stretched. The Strategic Petroleum Reserve sits near 325.7 million barrels, the lowest in four decades, after another 6.2 million barrel withdrawal last week even as commercial crude stayed roughly flat for the year. CRUDE OIL - WHAT IS NEXT?

I closed 75-80% of my Brent longs (long futures / sold puts / long UZ and VZ spreads) last week and today. Still keeping a decent amount of oil stocks (half - in smaller names, fairly levered to next 2 years prices, but with little or no debt).

Will probably get yelled at again by fellow oil investors :) - like it happened at the end of April.

Anyway, I was buying dips created by "deal is imminent" announcements by Trump & Co since March 9th, reducing risk in $100-120 area and it worked very well, except for the last time (MOU). I started adding way to early in June and had a larger than expected drawdown. While I ultimately made almost all the money back and overall am up quite a lot since the start of the war, this is not meant to be a victory lap - rather the review of what I got wrong.

The "long oil" trade always had two components:

1) geopolitical (Iran's leverage is proportionate to the tightness of the global oil market & maxed before US midterms / they also made no secret of the plan to have some degree of control over Hormuz). This part worked. I was surprised that the market started to price full recovery right after MOU despite vague language (that actually favoured Iran) and lack of real agreement on pretty much any substantial issue (nuclear / Hormuz). Felt wrong.

In reality it turned out that despite clause 5 of the MOU being much closer to "Iran controls the Strait" than to proper freedom of navigation, US had no intention to cede Hormuz (and the scale of US response was harsher than I anticipated). 

Geopolitical logic favouring long trade (at least from today's 87-91 levels) probably still stands, but I do not feel that I have any edge there. It seems that full normalisation is unlikely for quite a while, but there are tons of scenarios other than the escalation trap. The market could stay in limbo for some time - with partial, but still substantial flows or - eventually - after false starts and episodes of hostilities - come some type of arrangement that gives Iran partial, but not full control (joint management with Oman or other Gulf countries, perhaps in exchange to released funds/sanction relief etc). 

It is fairly clear that despite larger than expected escalation the parties are not targeting energy & export infra aggressively - there were some hits, but they were limited. So while hitting a port or a tanker is probably easier than attacking a US base, Iran has shown certain restraint there - which makes me think that the logic by which Iran will end up with full control because they *could* create a very large problem for oil production/exports does not necessarily work in a straightforward way.

I am not an expert there, so do not want to spend much time on that.

2) fundamental angle. Basically my original idea was that the scale of production loss is so large that the crude prices should stay relatively high at least till year end - and trades with breakeven in mid 80s seemed like a no brainer.

While I do not think now that it was/is necessarily wrong, the fundamental case has weakened.

Lots was said about China imports and flexibility of global refining system (managing to concentrate most of run cuts in petchem). There are a couple more factors that I underestimated:

a) Scale of refining bottleneck. There are 3 components there
- RU 2mbd+ refining losses due to drone attacks
- China. It looks like they are able to manage 2.5mbd refinery run cuts with just 600kbd drop in transport fuel production (which in itself reflects substitution). This is probably not sustainable long term as the petchem part of run cuts in part represents final product destocking, but I honestly do not think they will come aggressively bidding for crude at $90+. They also had around 1.9bln barrels between crude and products inventory (as per GS) before the war so could ease some run cuts by drawing  inventory more heavily

covered it here
https://t.co/HEi3hPznYM

- MEG refiners: it looks like product exports recovery could be lagging crude exports recovery - even desite crazy refining margins (see (b))

Add these 3 and it is easy to see how we ended up with record cracks, very high product prices (approaching demand destruction levels) and meh crude. I certainly underestimated the impact and e.g. closed ICE Gasoil spreads way too early leaving 3/4 of the money on the table.

b) Crude export recovery. It seems to me that the pre-MOU assumptions of the  about crude exports recovery were too conservative. 

"Hormuz shuttle" scheme - when risk-taking NOC (UAE mainly), Sinokor and greek tankers shuttle through Hormuz and S2S to more risk averse owners outside of the Strait really makes a difference here. At the same time there is no similar scheme for products - so product exports and MEG refinery outage recovery would be lagging. And the scheme and high risk environment raise freight costs and hurt premiums  but also benchmark crude prices indirectly (remember that China ultimately cares about landed price).

This again ties to refining bottleneck - if we go back to end of June environment with high refinery outages in China / RU /MEG and relatively large crude outflows from the Gulf we could have moderate crude prices even despite still large production outages, very high products and crazy cracks.

Anyway even though the fundamental bull case is certainly not dead (we do have a large inventory draw - EA estimated it at 650mb across products and crude at the end of June - incl. unobservable; I imagine it should be at least 700-750mb by end of July?;  I am sure that a large part of demand reduction is postponed demand, so it will come back), the trade now leans much stronger on geopolitics.

I do not have an angle there + having a very large long position that makes money on escalation screws with your incentives. To be clear - I do not think there is anything morally wrong with being long or short oil, but I really hate war and would rather sleep better when things are more peaceful ....

quotedTweet.text:
CHINA DEMAND

IEA report estimates that China demand dropped 1.6mbd YoY in May. Of thouse gasoline is down only 170kbd (but it was projected to drop 120kbd in the baseline pre-war 2026 demand forecast), diesel @ -330kbd vs projected growth of 45pbd. Jet was down 100kbd vs 46kbd projected growth.

Pre-war they had 2025 and 2026 demand growth of roughly 200kbd, so this means 1.8mbd from baseline.

With crude imports drop around 4mbd (vs average of 2025) and refinerly run cuts of 2.5mbd this implies circa 1.5-2mbd between pause in crude stockpiling (1mbd?) and crude draws (another 0.5mbd).  And another 0.7mbd product draws.

I am assuming above that local production growth YoY roughly matches the pre-war baseline demand growth (in reality it was slightly smaller, but within 100kbd).

Then part of the 1.8mbd demand drop - which is mainly concentrated in petchem - is a mix of pause in petchem product stockpiling and petchem products stock draws; ultimately in steady state at least petchem stock draws should reverse.

Overall, it seems to me that Chinese demand drop is much less dramatic, then is often portrayed - most of the adjustment were indeed stockpiling pause and stock draws, but since they were spread between visible and invisible crude storage, product storage (less observable) and petchem product storage (methodologically does not feed oil stock draws, as those are 1 step down the production chain) each component was either not that big or not visible.
